creator: wahjava@gmail.com
text:
Hi Heino,

Heino Tiedemann writes:
> Thank fÃ¼r the patch.

> can anyone tell me, how to install that patch?

Instead of applying that patch on ports tree, I recommend doing the following:

#v+
user@host ~ % mkdir ~/test-ports
user@host ~ % cd ~/test-ports
user@host ~/test-ports % cp -R /usr/ports/Mk .
user@host ~/test-ports % mkdir editors
user@host ~/test-ports % cd editors
user@host ~/test-ports/editors % cp -R /usr/ports/editors/emacs .
user@host ~/test-ports/editors % cd ~/test-ports
user@host ~/test-ports % patch -p1 </usr/home/rotkap/emacs-23.1-update.patch
user@host ~/test-ports % sudo make -C editors/emacs build deinstall install
#v-

And optionally, to build a package:

#v+
user@host ~/test-ports % sudo make -C editors/emacs package
#v-

And to cleanup:

#v+
user@host ~/test-ports % sudo make -C editors/emacs clean
#v-

Please adapt the instructions to your setup. After installing emacs port as
mentioned above, if you want to install rest of emacs related ports from ports
tree, you've to update bsd.emacs.mk in ports tree following additional steps
before installing them:

#v+
user@host ~/test-ports % sudo cp /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.emacs.mk /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.emacs.mk.orig
user@host ~/test-ports % sudo cp Mk/bsd.emacs.mk /usr/ports/Mk/emacs.mk /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.emacs.mk
#v-

To revert back to original bsd.emacs.mk

#v+
user@host ~/test-ports % sudo cp /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.emacs.mk.orig /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.emacs.mk
#v-

I've not tried above instructions so I'm not sure if they'll work as mentioned.

HTH
-- 
Ashish SHUKLA
